description: Stable row identifier for a source record.
Exact provenance of extracted row.
Normalized/corrected citation text used for DOI extraction and publication grouping.
Original citation text from the source workbook when preserved separately from normalized citation text.
Secondary compilation source metadata, used when values entered through a peer-reviewed meta-analysis workbook rather than direct primary-paper extraction.
Original reference text from the secondary workbook before DOI resolution.
Crossref/OpenAlex title-year resolution status and match score for secondary workbook references.
Higher-priority record that appears to duplicate this lower-priority secondary row.
Value as represented in source workbook after basic null cleaning.
Canonical density.
Canonical specific volume calculated from density.
Raw tenacity/specific strength from source.
Canonical specific strength; 1 N/tex = 1e6 N m/kg.
Canonical tensile strength in GPa after scale inference.
Canonical tensile strength.
Canonical electrical conductivity; S/cm multiplied by 100 or MS/m by 1e6.
Electrical conductivity divided by density.
Thermal conductivity.
Thermal conductivity divided by density.
Maximum current density before open circuit/failure.
Initial comparison classification for UI filtering.
Citation/provenance category used for public badges and source filters.
Evidence strength tier distinguishing primary literature records from contextual benchmark comparators.
Human-readable disclosure text for website tooltips and figure legends.
Public exposure state. Seed rows are not marked public-official until curation/validation is complete.
Internal curation state for reference, unit, condition, and value review.
Nullable measurement-condition metadata. Missing values block strict scientific comparison.
Formal citation year when known.
Online/version-of-record publication date when known.
Separate derived plot-series table from RadarFigureSource/FibersStrengthYr.
Rows deliberately excluded from scientific records because they are plot headers, max values, scores, or unlabeled plot cells.
